verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass transmembrane protein (Ig-like fold ectodomain) that is essential for sperm-oocyte plasma membrane fusion. It is expressed exclusively on mature sperm — a highly restricted germline/gamete cell type — where it is displayed on the outer leaflet of the sperm plasma membrane. Despite possessing its own TM anchor, expression is confined to sperm, qualifying as tissue_restricted_surface (gamete-lineage-restricted) rather than broadly expressed surface receptor. The protein body is unambiguously at the extracellular face during fertilization, making it accessible to binders, but only in this narrow developmental/germline context.
